entries.intro: Saw something big moving through the trees past the ridge. Too far off to tell what it was.
details: It was tall. Taller than a deer. Moved slow, though. Didn't seem to be in a hurry.
By the time I squinted hard enough it was gone behind the ridge.
reactions: Probably an elk or something. My eyes aren't what they used to be.

statCheck.pass: The shape was wrong for a deer. Too broad in the shoulders. Might've been a bear coming out of its den early.
statCheck.fail: Could've been anything, honestly. At that distance, a tall bush looks like a creature.

entries.intro: The birds have been acting strange lately. All flying the same direction, like they're avoiding something.
details: I noticed it a few mornings in a row. They come off the roosts and bank hard to the south.
A friend of mine said he saw the same thing. Neither of us knows what to make of it.
reactions: Birds know things we don't. Or they're just birds and I'm reading too much into it.

statCheck.pass: Sometimes a nesting predator will clear out the smaller birds from an area. Hawk, maybe, or a large owl.
statCheck.fail: I don't know the first thing about birds. They fly. That's about all I've got.

entries.intro: Found some tracks out by the tree line that I didn't recognize. Bigger than a dog's, but the wrong shape.
details: The mud was soft, so the prints were clear. Four toes, deep heel. Something heavy.
I looked around for more but the ground dried out past the tree line.
reactions: I showed a friend and he said it's just a wolf. Maybe he's right.

entries.intro: My cousin swears she saw lights out past the fields after sunset. Little ones, low to the ground.
details: She described them as flickering, like candles. But there's nobody living out that way.
I told her it was fireflies and she gave me a look that could curdle milk.
reactions: I believe she saw something. I just don't think it's as strange as she wants it to be.

statCheck.pass: Foxfire does that. Rotting wood glows sometimes. Especially after wet weather when the fungus is active.
statCheck.fail: Couldn't tell you what it was. Lights are lights.

entries.intro: I spotted a family of foxes near the old fence yesterday. Three kits tumbling around in the grass.
details: The mother just sat there watching them. Didn't even flinch when I walked past.
One of the little ones chased its own tail for a solid minute. I stood there like a fool, grinning.
reactions: Best thing I've seen all week. Sometimes you just need a reminder that the world is doing fine without you.
